title: Recovering the three-dimensional atomic structure of nanoparticles in the presence of atomic diffusion in scanning transmission electron microscopy
author: Stoops, Tom
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
kinetic_Monte_Carlo_time_evolutions.zip  contains all time evolutions where time steps correspond to pixel measurements in ADF-STEM, stored in lammpstrj-files for compatibility with various viewers. 
  median_nanoparticles.zip  contain the coordinates of the median nanoparticles, calculated from their corresponding time evolutions. 
  simulated_images.zip  contains subdirectories named  dose_1e3 , ...,  dose_1e5 , and  dose_Inf  which contain the simulated ADF-STEM images using the time evolutions for their corresponding simulated electron dose measured in electrons per squared Angstrom. (Infinite dose is noise-free.) 
  bga_reconstructions.zip  contains subdirectories named after the applicable doses which contain the coordinates of the BGA reconstructions of the nanoparticles for all analysed images. 
  surface_fractions.zip  contains the calculated recovered surface fractions as mat-files. The mat-files contain arrays of recovered surface fractions, for various comparisons:  
 
  surffracs_bga_median.mat  compares the reconstructed BGA structures to the median structures recovered from the time evolutions. The array is a 6x32 double, for the 6 considered doses (1e3, ..., inf) and the 32 time evolutions. 
  surffracs_time_median.mat  compares the time evolution steps to the median structures which were recovered from their respective time evolutions. The array is a 65536x32 double, for the 65536 time steps in the time evolution and the 32 time evolutions. 
  surffracs_time_bga.mat  compares the time evolution steps to the reconstructed BGA structures. The array is a 65536x32x6 double, for the 65536 time steps, 6 considered doses, and 32 time evolutions. 
 
 Additional analyses were carried out in which 8 additional scalings of the time evolution were simulated. For these 8 new values, 5 new time evolutions were generated using the kinetic Monte Carlo method. For these 40 time evolutions, all 6 previously considered doses were again applied prior to analysis. The data is zipped in the file  additional_analyses_timescaling.zip  with the same

For these 8 new values, 5 new time evolutions were generated using the kinetic Monte Carlo method.
